skill.name: widgets-ui
skill.description: Declarative UI widgets from JSON for React/Next.js from ui.inference.sh. Render rich interactive UIs from structured agent responses. Capabilities: forms, buttons, cards, layouts, inputs, selects, checkboxes. Use for: agent-generated UIs, dynamic forms, data display, interactive cards. Triggers: widgets, declarative ui, json ui, widget renderer, agent widgets, dynamic ui, form widgets, card widgets, shadcn widgets, structured output ui
skill.summary: Declarative UI widgets from JSON for React/Next.js from ui.inference.sh. Render rich interactive UIs...
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: All 34 static findings are false positives from documentation patterns. The external_commands detections are TypeScript code examples in markdown blocks, not executable commands. Network URLs are legitimate project documentation links. No cryptographic weakness exists - flags were triggered by markdown table syntax. This is pure documentation for a React component library with no executable code.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: All network URLs are legitimate project documentation and resource links. Lines 8, 10, 15, 73, 168-172 reference official inference.sh documentation and package repositories. These are documentation references, not executable network calls.

content.user_title: Generate Declarative UI Widgets from JSON
content.value_statement: Agents struggle to render complex user interfaces. This skill lets Claude generate rich interactive React components from simple JSON structures.
actual_capabilities: Generate React/Next.js UI components from JSON schemas
Create interactive forms with inputs, textareas, selects, and checkboxes
Build responsive layouts with rows, columns, and boxes
Display rich content with badges, icons, images, and typography
Handle user actions through configurable event handlers
Apply predefined gradients and styling variants
limitations: Requires shadcn/ui installation in your project
Only supports predefined widget types and properties
Custom styling limited to available variants and gradients
Client-side rendering only, no server component support

use_cases.title: Agent-Generated Forms
use_cases.description: Claude creates dynamic multi-step forms based on user requirements. The agent generates JSON widget definitions that render as complete forms with validation.

use_cases.title: Data Visualization Cards
use_cases.description: Transform structured data into visual cards with icons, badges, and layouts. Display flight information, product details, or user profiles.

use_cases.title: Interactive Dashboards
use_cases.description: Build dashboard interfaces by combining multiple widgets. Create rows and columns of buttons, stats, and data displays.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Contact Form
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a contact form widget with name, email, and message fields. Add a submit button with default variant.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Product Card
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a product card widget with an ocean gradient background. Include a product title, price badge, and purchase button.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Flight Status Display
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a flight status widget showing departure and arrival airports in a row layout. Add a plane icon between the locations.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Multi-Section Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a column layout with three sections: a header with title, a form with email and password inputs, and a footer with copyright text.

output_examples.input: Create a login form with email and password fields
output_examples.output: Widget JSON with type 'ui', asForm set to true, containing a column with email input, password input, and submit button

output_examples.input: Make a notification card with success badge
output_examples.output: Box widget with gradient-ocean background, padding 4, radius lg, containing a title, success badge, and close button

best_practices: Always define the 'type' field as 'ui' for root widgets to enable proper rendering
Use semantic names for form inputs (email, password, message) to improve form data handling
Combine box and row/column layouts to create responsive, structured interfaces
Leverage predefined gradients like 'gradient-ocean' and 'gradient-sunset' for consistent styling
anti_patterns: Do not nest widgets deeper than 4 levels as this complicates maintenance and performance
Avoid mixing different layout types (row and column) without clear structural purpose
Never include sensitive data in widget JSON as it renders client-side
Do not use custom properties outside the defined widget schema as they will be ignored

faq.question: What frameworks does this skill work with?
faq.answer: This skill works with React and Next.js projects that have shadcn/ui installed.

faq.question: How do I install the required components?
faq.answer: Run the installation command: npx shadcn@latest add https://ui.inference.sh/r/widgets.json

faq.question: Can I customize the widget styles?
faq.answer: Styles use predefined variants and gradients. Custom CSS requires modifying the component implementation.

faq.question: What widget types are supported?
faq.answer: Layout types: row, col, box. Typography: title, text, caption, label. Interactive: button, input, textarea, select, checkbox. Display: badge, icon, image, divider.

faq.question: How do I handle form submissions?
faq.answer: Set asForm to true on the root widget and implement the onAction handler to receive form data when submit button is clicked.

faq.question: Can I use this in server components?
faq.answer: No, widgets require client-side rendering. Use 'use client' directive in components that render widgets.
